title: ADP storage map
description: A user-held mapping from the logical store aliases used in a manifest's parts[].location.store (`default`, `cold`, and each `byos:<alias>`) to a real storage endpoint. This file is NOT part of the package and never travels with it: the manifest carries only logical aliases so it leaks no account, location, or credential. Secrets are NEVER inline — a store references credentials by name only (`credentials_ref`), resolved out-of-band by the reader (env var, keychain handle, cloud profile, etc.).

stores.description: Alias -> store. Keys are the aliases used in manifest location.store: the literal `default`, the literal `cold`, and each `<alias>` from a `byos:<alias>` token (the `byos:` prefix is stripped when looking up here).

store.description: One resolved storage endpoint. `kind: local` endpoints are a filesystem base path the reader can read directly; all other kinds are remote and carry a `credentials_ref` (name of a secret held elsewhere), never the secret itself.

kind.description: Backend kind. `local` = a filesystem base path; the rest are remote object stores / URLs.

endpoint.description: Base location. For `local`, a filesystem directory (part keys resolve under it). For remote kinds, the bucket/base URL (e.g. s3://my-bucket, https://host/prefix).

prefix.description: OPTIONAL key prefix prepended to each part key within this store.

credentials_ref.description: OPTIONAL name/handle of the credential to use, resolved out-of-band (e.g. an env var name, an AWS profile, a keychain entry). MUST NOT be an inline secret. Omit for public or unauthenticated endpoints.

cold.description: OPTIONAL: this store is archival and may need rehydration before its objects are readable.
